summary: PR 2483 decompiled and typed more of the lbcardnew memory-card snapshot path. It replaced the previous stub for lb_8001B14C with a partial snapshot scanner, introduced a shared lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry type, propagated meaningful snapshot/free-space names into lbsnap, refactored card-entry descriptor setup, and completed four near-matching lbcardnew functions per the decomp.dev report. The PR had no body or human review comments, so intent beyond the diff and bot match report is weak.

key_files.role: Main decomp work: CardEntry helper, lb_8001B14C snapshot scan implementation, typed lb_8001BFD8 outputs, and wrapper/task setup changes.
Adds lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry with time, file_no, blocks and renames lb_80432A68_t snapshot/free-space fields.
Publishes the typed lb_8001BFD8 signature using lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry and int* free-space outputs.
Adds the forward typedef for lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry.
Updates snapshot consumers to use card_result, free_blocks, free_files, entries, time, file_no, and blocks names.
Replaces the local anonymous snapshot-entry struct with lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry and keeps the slot-state size assert.
Renames hsd_803AC3E0 arguments to file_size, file_flags, and data_size and updates descriptor assignments.
Keeps the hsd_803AC3E0 prototype aligned with the new semantic argument names.
Reorders existing warning suppressions and adds -Wno-format.

what_changed: Implemented lb_8001B14C beyond its previous stub. The new code scans memory-card file numbers 0..0x7E with CARDGetStatus, filters files by current DVD company/game and digit-starting filename, parses the filename as a decimal timestamp with strtoul, records file_no and blocks from stat.length >> 13, sorts found snapshots by descending time, copies them to snapshot_entries, marks remaining entries with file_no = -1, and frees the temporary node buffer.

Changed lb_8001BFD8 from UNK_T snapshot arguments to int lb_8001BFD8(int chan, lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries, int* free_blocks, int* free_files), then stores those pointers in lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries, free_blocks, and free_files.
Added struct lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry in lb/types.h with fields time, file_no, and blocks plus STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x8).
Renamed lb_80432A68_t fields at offsets 0x20, 0x24, and 0x28 from unk_20/unk_24/unk_28 to snapshot_entries/free_blocks/free_files.
Renamed lbsnap slot-state fields from unk0, unk8, unkC, and unk10 to card_result, free_blocks, free_files, and entries, and updated all users to access entries[index].time/file_no/blocks.
Introduced struct CardEntry with file_size, file_flags, and data_size and an inline setup_card_entries helper that wraps hsd_803B2ADC plus the hsd_803AC3E0 loop over sentinel-terminated entries.
Updated lb_8001A594, lb_8001ACEC, lb_8001AE38, and lb_8001C4A8 to use CardEntry field names instead of anonymous unk0/unk4/unk8 triplets.

Expanded lb_8001BA44 and lb_8001BC18 into explicit task setup sequences using setup_task, lb_8001A4CC_dontinline, strncpy or memcpy filename copies, stack padding arrays, and lb_80019CB0 instead of delegating to inline helper calls.
Changed hsd_803AC3E0 parameter names and call semantics to file_size, file_flags, and data_size, with descriptor writes to x28, x4C, and x70 reflecting those names.
Other touched files such as lb_01F8.c, lbmthp.c, mndatadel.c, and mndatadel.static.h are mostly formatting or include-order cleanup in the shown diff.
smart_moves: Shared lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry between lbcardnew and lbsnap instead of keeping a private duplicate snapshot struct, which made the lb_8001BFD8 API and lbsnap consumers self-documenting while preserving the 0x8 layout.
Used consumer behavior in lbsnap to name the card scan outputs: card_result, free_blocks, free_files, entries, time, file_no, and blocks.
Centralized repeated card file descriptor setup in setup_card_entries, making the sentinel table convention explicit and reducing duplicated hsd_803B2ADC/hsd_803AC3E0 loops.
Kept exact matching-oriented details in small wrappers, including stack padding arrays, specific setup_task constants, dontinline helper use, and strncpy versus memcpy choices.
Improved baselib hsd_803AC3E0 argument names at the producer so later call-site decompilation can reason about file_size, file_flags, and data_size consistently.
Replaced a pure stub with a semantically useful partial implementation for lb_8001B14C while the bot report still marked it as not fully matched, giving future work a concrete behavioral skeleton.
decomp_lessons: For lbcardnew memory-card snapshot scans, filenames are treated as decimal timestamps: lb_8001B14C uses isdigit on fileName[0] and strtoul(fileName, NULL, 10).
Snapshot scan entries are filtered to the current disc by comparing CARDStat company and gameName against DVDGetCurrentDiskID company and gameName.
Snapshot entries use an 0x8 layout: u32 time, s16 file_no, u16 blocks. The sentinel for unused entries is file_no = -1.
Free memory-card blocks are computed from lb_80432A68.unused_bytes / 0x2000, and per-file blocks are computed from CARDStat.length >> 13.
The temporary SnapshotNode array size 0x5F4 corresponds to 0x7F nodes of 0xC bytes, matching the scan range of 0x7F card file slots.
CardEntry tables are sentinel-terminated with file_size == -1, skip entries with file_size == 0, and pass file_size, file_flags, and data_size into hsd_803AC3E0.
When matching tiny card-operation wrapper functions, expanding an inline wrapper back into the exact task queue setup can recover the last few nonmatching bytes.
Use STATIC_ASSERTs when replacing unk structs with named shared structs; this PR preserved lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry at 0x8 and Unk80433380_48 at 0x408.
naming_conventions: New shared lb types use a module-prefixed name: lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry.
Snapshot-entry field names are time, file_no, and blocks.
lbsnap slot state now uses card_result, num, free_blocks, free_files, and entries instead of unk-style names.
lb_80432A68_t output pointers are named snapshot_entries, free_blocks, and free_files.
Card file table entries are named CardEntry with file_size, file_flags, and data_size.
The local linked-list helper for the scan is named SnapshotNode with next, time, file_no, and blocks.
Address-based function names such as lb_8001B14C and lb_8001BFD8 were retained; the old stub comment citing UnclePunch's MemoryCard_ScanForSnapshots name was removed when code was added.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: lb_8001BA44 uses an explicit sequence: lb_80019EF0, setup_task(0, 0x10000), setup_task(1, 0x201), lb_8001A4CC_dontinline, setup_task(3, -1), strncpy into setup_task(5, 0xE)->x10, then lb_80019CB0(0x10).
lb_8001BC18 similarly spells out setup_task calls, uses a local new_var = 0x20, memcpy into setup_task(7, 0x10)->x10, and writes lb_80432A68.unk_14/unk_18/unk_1C before lb_80019CB0.
Stack padding arrays u8 _[0x10] and u8 _[0x18] were added in wrappers to shape the generated stack frame.
lb_8001C4A8 introduces local temporaries void* icon and s32* ctx plus a scoped inner int i block, indicating local ordering/scope was used for codegen matching.
The helper setup_card_entries is declared inline, while lb_8001A4CC_dontinline remains used in wrapper setup, showing deliberate inline/noinline control.
lb_8001B14C uses a pointer-to-pointer insertion scan over SnapshotNode** to build a sorted linked list without a separate sort pass.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 66.59%, +0.04%, +1428 bytes.
decomp-dev bot reported four new matches in main/melee/lb/lbcardnew: lb_8001C4A8 to 100.00%, lb_8001A594 to 100.00%, lb_8001BA44 to 100.00%, and lb_8001BC18 to 100.00%.
decomp-dev bot reported lb_8001B14C improved from 1.04% to 63.23% but remained unmatched.
